releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release 1.12.10

## Summary
- Escaped OAuth callback response HTML and added callback regression coverage.
- Redacted sensitive prompt/provider/log payloads and bounded note-content prompt injection surfaces.
- Preserved mobile manifest support while gating Node-only streaming paths to desktop runtime.

releases.author: GoBeromsu
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Refactoring
- **Unified Provider Authentication System**: OAuth and API Key authentication now use a common `ProviderAuth` union type
- **Reorganized Auth Module**: Moved `src/auth/` to `src/provider/auth/` for better code organization
- **Integrated Settings UI**: Removed separate `PlanConnectionsSection` and integrated OAuth configuration into `ProviderModal`
- **Settings Migration**: Automatic migration of legacy `codexConnection` settings to new format

### Testing
- Added comprehensive test coverage for auth module (token-manager, pkce, oauth)
- Added ClassificationService tests for auth validation
- Added ProviderSection tests for OAuth status display
- Test count increased from 115 to 194 tests

### Technical Details
- Added `ProviderAuth` union type: `{ type: 'apiKey'; apiKey: string } | { type: 'oauth'; oauth: OAuthTokens }`
- Added `getApiKey()` helper in `UnifiedProvider` for backward compatibility
- Added `hasValidAuth()` method in `ClassificationService`
- Added `migrateSettings()` in main plugin for automatic migration

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/GoBeromsu/Metadata-Auto-Classifier/compare/1.7.1...1.8.0

releases.author: GoBeromsu
releases.description:
## Codex OAuth Authentication

### Features
- Add Codex (ChatGPT Pro) OAuth authentication
- Plan Connections section in settings  
- PKCE-based OAuth flow with local callback server
- Automatic token refresh on plugin load
- Auto-add/remove Codex provider on connect/disconnect

**Note:** Desktop only (requires browser for OAuth redirect)

releases.author: GoBeromsu
releases.description:
## Architecture Refactoring

This release includes a comprehensive codebase restructuring to improve maintainability and developer experience.

### Changes

- **Consolidated types and constants**: All shared types and constants are now in `src/types.ts` and `src/constants.ts`
- **Renamed folders for clarity**:
  - `api/` → `provider/` (AI provider logic)
  - `services/` → `classifier/` (classification business logic)
  - `utils/` → `lib/` (shared utilities)
  - `ui/` → `settings/` (settings UI)
- **Flattened folder structure**: Reduced maximum folder depth from 3 to 2 levels
- **Updated path aliases**: All imports use clear, consistent path aliases

### Breaking Changes

None - this is a refactoring release with no changes to functionality.

---
Generated by Claude Code

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
I’ve fixed the API format issue in Gemini, but there is still a critical limitation.

### ⚠️ Gemini Structured Output Warning

Gemini's `response_schema` does **not enforce hard constraints**. Even with a valid JSON schema (e.g., `array<string>`, `number` with bounds), Gemini may still return structurally or semantically incorrect outputs:

- Schema type names as literal values (e.g., `"output": ["string"]`)
- Out-of-bound numbers (e.g., `"reliability": 1.23` when max is 1)

#### Root Cause

Gemini treats `response_schema` as a **guideline**, not a validator. The model does **not strictly adhere to**:
- `type`, `items`, `required`
- `minimum`, `maximum` for numeric values
- Natural language rules in `description`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 📢 Feature Update: Custom Prompts for Frontmatter Classification

## What's New

We're excited to announce a significant improvement to the Metadata Auto-Classifier plugin: **Custom Classification Prompts per Frontmatter**!

### Key Improvements

- **Per-Frontmatter Custom Prompts**: You can now specify custom classification instructions for each frontmatter, allowing for more precise and tailored classifications based on specific content types.

- **Improved Prompt Structure**: We've restructured our prompt templates using explicit XML-style tags (`<task>`, `<instructions>`, etc.) following OpenAI's best practices for more reliable and consistent results.

- **Better Classification Context**: The renamed `<classification_context>` tag (previously `<custom_rules>`) provides clearer guidance to the AI about specific classification requirements.

- **Separated Tag Count Instructions**: Tag count limits are now specified in a dedicated section for better clarity.

- **Enhanced Hierarchical Support**: Improved support for nested categories (e.g., category/subcategory) with explicit instructions.

## How It Benefits You

- **More Accurate Classifications**: Custom prompts tailored to specific content types lead to more relevant tag suggestions.
  
- **Greater Control**: Fine-tune how the AI interprets and classifies different types of content in your vault.
  
- **Flexibility**: Different notes can have different classification guidelines based on their purpose or subject matter.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Configuration System Enhancement

We've significantly improved the plugin's configuration management system, focusing on seamless model updates and configuration persistence:

### Key Improvements

- **Enhanced Settings Merge Logic**
  - New AI models now automatically appear for existing users
  - Configuration system intelligently preserves user settings while updating provider models
  - GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and GPT-4.1 nano models added to the available options

- **Better Type Safety**
  - Implemented generic type system with proper interfaces
  - Reduced potential for runtime errors through stronger typing
  - Improved code editor support with better type hints

- **Code Quality Enhancements**
  - Adopted immutability principles for more predictable behavior
  - Separated concerns with modular helper functions
  - Improved documentation with clear English comments

### User Experience Benefits

- Existing users receive new AI model options without manual intervention
- Personal settings (API keys, custom URLs) are preserved during updates
- Enhanced stability and reliability when loading configurations

This update ensures all users can immediately access the latest OpenAI models while maintaining their custom settings.

- **OpenRouter Integration**: Added support for OpenRouter API, allowing access to 300+ AI models through a single API

  - Google Gemma 3 27B
  - Google Gemini 2.0 Flash
  - Meta Llama 3.3 70B
  - DeepSeek R1
- **Auto Routing**: Added support for OpenRouter's auto routing capability

### Technical Changes
- Added OpenRouter provider class with authentication and response handling
- Implemented proper structured output format with strict schema validation
- Updated provider selection system to include OpenRouter alongside existing options

🎉 Release Notes: Frontmatter Processing Enhancement

### 🔄 Major Refactoring
- ♻️ Simplified frontmatter value processing logic
- 🎯 Improved WikiLink format handling

### ✨ Key Improvements
- 🔗 Enhanced WikiLink format preservation
  - WikiLinks are now consistently maintained in `[[link]]` format
  - Better handling of existing WikiLink structures
- 📝 Raw value processing
  - Normal values are stored in their original format
  - WikiLinks are properly formatted during input/output
- 🧹 Code cleanup
  - Removed redundant string processing functions
  - Streamlined frontmatter update logic

### 🛠️ Technical Details
- Removed unnecessary string manipulation functions (`processString`, `formatStringByLinkType`)
- Simplified frontmatter value storage and display logic
- Improved format consistency across the application

### 🎨 UI Updates
- Better visualization of WikiLinks in the settings interface
- Consistent formatting in textarea displays

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🚀 Release Notes - v1.3.002

## ✨ UI Enhancements

- **📱 Modernized Layout & Design**: Completely revamped CSS with improved spacing, consistent colors, and modern interactions for a more polished experience
- **🎛️ Reorganized Controls**: Moved delete button to the controls row for more intuitive organization alongside Link Type, Overwrite, and Count settings
- **📏 Optimized Component Widths**: Fixed issues where text was getting cut off in narrow containers
- **🌓 Improved Visual Contrast**: Enhanced visual hierarchy with better backgrounds, shadows, and hover states

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **🔗 WikiLink Spacing Fix**: Resolved issue where spaces in WikiLinks (e.g., `[[🏷 Daily Notes]]`) were incorrectly removed
- **✏️ Frontmatter Name Updates**: Fixed Frontmatter name not immediately updating when edited
- **🎯 Command Palette Integration**: Command palette now immediately reflects newly added frontmatter settings

## 💅 Design Improvements

- **🎨 Consistent Color Scheme**: Implemented a unified color system using CSS variables for better theme compatibility
- **📊 Responsive Design**: Added responsive layouts that adapt to different screen sizes
- **⚡ Interactive Elements**: Enhanced hover and focus states for all interactive components
- **❓ Improved Tooltips**: Redesigned tooltips for better readability and contextual help

## 🧰 Technical Improvements

- **🔄 Refactored CSS**: Removed redundant code and organized styling with modern best practices
- **📦 Better Encapsulation**: Improved component structure for maintainability
- **🔍 Accessibility Enhancements**: Better focus indicators and semantic structure

## Refactoring and Improvements

1. **Removed Unused Import**: 
   - Cleaned up the codebase by removing an unnecessary import.

2. **Repositioned Delete Button**:
   - Moved the delete button to improve the user interface and functionality.

3. **Removed Frontmatter Name**:

4. **Updated Tag Logic**:

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### OpenAI JSON Mode Integration
- Added support for OpenAI's JSON mode, enhancing the structure and reliability of API responses.
- Improved parsing and handling of API outputs, resulting in more consistent and predictable data processing.

### Automatic Tag Update on Plugin Load
- Implemented automatic tag updating when the plugin is loaded.
- This ensures that all tags are up-to-date and properly synchronized with the latest plugin state, improving overall functionality and user experience.

These updates aim to enhance the plugin's performance, reliability, and ease of use. Users can expect more structured data from OpenAI interactions and a more seamless experience with tag management.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Major Changes

#### Refactoring

- **Separated Handler Dependencies and Eliminated Circular References**
  - Clearly defined the responsibilities of `FrontMatterHandler`, `APIHandler`, and `MetaDataHandler`.
  - Restructured code to ensure each handler focuses on its specific role.
  - Resolved circular dependencies to enhance stability and maintainability.

- **Refactored Command Setup Logic**
  - Moved command setup into a dedicated method for improved readability.
  - Simplifies future additions and modifications of commands.

#### New Features

- **Implemented Global Error Handler**
  - Introduced a consistent error handling mechanism across the plugin.
  - Streamlines debugging and accelerates issue resolution.
  - Provides users with clearer and more informative error messages.

### Other Improvements

- Optimized performance through code enhancements.
- Clarified component responsibilities to ease future development and maintenance.

### Developer Notes

- Updated handler interfaces may require code adjustments if you're using previous versions.
- Please acquaint yourself with the new error handling methodology.

This update reinforces the plugin's architecture, paving the way for more efficient development and maintenance. Your feedback is valuable to us.
